title: Adesmia berteroi
intro: Adesmia berteroi, commonly known as Quebracho Colorado, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Adesmia berteroi include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Adesmia berteroi include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat pain, fever, and respiratory issues.

side_effects: Adesmia berteroi is a plant that, like many plants in the Fabaceae family, may have side effects due to its potential toxicity.
